statement: The highest residual run risk is not duplicate apply but scale-driven read pressure and scheduler backlog under fanout.
rationale: Idempotency/lease guards are mature and tested; prior incidents concentrated around large-volume scans and queue/scheduler behavior.
keyAssumptions: duplicate apply tests reflect production code paths
run-scale fanout remains the dominant load amplifier
validationCriteria: no read-limit errors during medium fanout canaries
critiquePoints: Partial-stage semantics can hide quality degradation
questions: Do we need stage-specific SLA limits before full matrix?
